channel.title: Home on M. Sten Delos
channel.description: Recent content in Home on M. Sten Delos

item.title: Formation of halos prior to the matter epoch
item.description: Related Publications Ultradense dark matter haloes accompany primordial black holes (2022) Annihilation signatures of hidden sector dark matter within early-forming microhalos (2019) [Back]

item.title: Probing the early universe using dark matter minihalos
item.description: In the very first second, the Universe is thought to have undergone a period of accelerated expansion. During this cosmic inflation, tiny quantum fluctuations expanded to much larger size, seeding initially minor variations in the density of the Universe. Subsequently, regions that were slightly overdense attracted surrounding mass, becoming still denser and eventually growing into the galaxies that we see today. Inflation solves several problems in Big Bang cosmology, such as why opposite corners of the sky have the same temperature despite never coming into causal contact.

item.title: Prompt cusps of the first halos
item.description: The first dark matter halos formed around redshift 30, at a time of about 100 million years. These potentially earth-mass objects are the basis for all larger structure, but their formation mechanism is special because they arise from the smooth mass distribution of the early universe. Immediately at formation, each halo develops a central structure in which the density scales with radius as ρ ~ r^-1.5. The properties of these prompt cusp features, such as their sizes and masses, can be predicted accurately and straightforwardly from the initial conditions without need for numerical simulations.

item.title: Predicting populations of dark matter halos
item.description: Related Publications Evolution of dark matter microhalos through stellar encounters (2019) Tidal evolution of dark matter annihilation rates in subhalos (2019) Predicting the density profiles of the first halos (2019) [Back]

item.title: Stochastic gravitational influence of dark halos
item.description: Related Publications Stellar streams and dark substructure: the diffusion regime (2022) [Back]

item.title: Other
item.description: gadget4-tools: A repository containing Python scripts to handle the creation and analysis of GADGET-4 simulation snapshots. Solutions to past UNC Physics qualifying exams My dissertation [Talks]

item.title: Past UNC Physics Qualifier Solutions
item.description: Below are my solutions to a range of past UNC-Chapel Hill physics qualifying exam problems. This is intended as a resource for other UNC-Chapel Hill physics graduate students. Be warned that not all solutions are correct! If you find something wrong, let me know and I'll make a note of it. Also, I have no solutions to the astro problems, but all other missing solutions are noted. 2015 exam 2015 qualifying exam

item.title: Publications
item.description: Here are lists of my publications: arXiv preprints ADS list INSPIRE list (INSPIRE profile) My ORCID [Research] [Talks]

item.title: Talks
item.description: This is a representative subset of my talks. Prompt cusps and the dark matter annihilation signal [PDF] (SF1258 Neutrinos and Dark Matter colloquium—October 17, 2022) Stellar streams and dark substructure [PDF] (Munich Dark Matter Meeting—March 8, 2022) Observational signatures of early matter domination [PDF] (Particle Seminar, Carleton University—September 27, 2021) Probing cosmology using dark matter microhalos [PPT] [PDF] (Perimeter Institute for Theoretical Physics—February 6, 2020) The gamma-ray signature of an early matter-dominated era [PPT] [PDF] (APS April Meeting, 2019)
